temporal_description:
A close-up shot focuses on a woman with highlighted brown hair lying in a hospital bed. She wears a white patterned hospital gown and looks up with a concerned and slightly distressed expression. A man in a light-colored shirt is visible on the right, leaning over her and speaking. The background is a simple hospital room with light purple walls and a patterned pillow behind the woman. The audio begins with a woman speaking in a soft, slightly weak, and questioning voice, as if she's in a vulnerable or tired state. She says, "at me?" Her line is immediately followed by the sound of a live studio audience laughing.

The camera pulls back to a wider shot, revealing more of the hospital room. The woman is in the bed, and the man stands beside her, looking down with a worried expression. In the background, another man in a plaid shirt is sitting on the edge of the bed, looking up at the standing man. The room is equipped with medical equipment, including an IV stand with bags hanging from it, and a curtain is visible in the background. A man then speaks in a loud, firm, and slightly exasperated tone, as if trying to get someone's attention and make a point. He exclaims, "Hey! You want to live to see your baby?"

The camera angle shifts, focusing on the man in the plaid shirt. He is now looking up and to his left, speaking with an animated and concerned expression. The man in the light-colored shirt stands in the background, slightly out of focus. The curtain and the medical equipment are still visible in the background of this shot.
